title: Tibouchina dubia
taxonomy: Tibouchina dubia is classified within the family Melastomataceae. This family consists of approximately 170 genera and 5,000 species of flowering plants. Tibouchina dubia is a species of flowering plant in the genus Tibouchina, which is native to South America. The genus Tibouchina is further classified in the tribe Miconieae and subfamily Miconioideae. Tibouchina dubia is a member of the order Myrtales.
uses: Tibouchina dubia is a species known for its potential medicinal properties, with research suggesting its uses in treating various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever and infections, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Additionally, Tibouchina dubia may possess anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in managing pain and swelling. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, making it a subject of interest in the development of new antimicrobial agents. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Tibouchina dubia.
